MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Relief To Serve Late Demand For Disclosure Of Expert Witnesses
Off Calendar Aug-18-2022 Advanced From Sep-07-22 To Aug-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Relief To Serve Late Demand For Disclosure Of Expert Witnesses
Off Calendar Aug-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JENNIFER WANG, Esq. of Open Door Legal (Tel: 415-323-0946) for plaintiff Contessa Osborne;
Atty. NICK O'BRIEN-KOVARI, Esq. of Freeman Mathis & Gary, LLP (Tel: 925-466-2500) for defendant Costco Wholesale Corporation.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION's Motion For Relief To Serve Late Demand For Disclosure Of Expert Witnesses.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications, as follows:

Defendants Motion for Relief to Serve Late Demand for Disclosure of Expert Witnesses is GRANTED. The Court finds good cause based upon excusable neglect arising from a calendaring error. "In examining the mistake or neglect, the court inquires whether "a reasonably prudent person under the same or similar circumstances" might have made the same error. (Ebersol, supra, 35 Cal.3d at p. 435, citing Tammen v. County of San Diego (1967) 66 Cal. 2d 468, 476 [58 Cal. Rptr. 249, 426 P.2d 753].) In addition, "[u]nless inexcusable neglect is clear, the policy favoring trial on the merits prevails." (Elston, supra, 38 Cal.3d at p. 235.)" Bettencourt v. Los Rios College Dist. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 270, 276) Plaintiff's claim of prejudice is belied by her own papers wherein she suggests that she would wait until the eve of trial to decide whom to call as an expert. (See, Plaintiff's Opposition at 6:15-17) Courts in every jurisdiction in California disapprove the concepts of "sandbagging" and "trial by ambush". It inures to the benefit of both sides and to the fair conduct of trials to know what witnesses each side will rely upon in presenting their case. The parties are ORDERED to exchange retained and non-retained expert witness information before the close of business on September 19, 2022. Defendant will be provided five days to make a supplemental disclosure only if plaintiff were to disclose a retained expert that defendant did not anticipate. The expert discovery deadline for conducting expert discovery (not disclosures) is to run from the new trial date set by the trial department once the Presiding Judge has assigned this case to a trial department.

Counsel for parties are to meet and confer as to a proposed order and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at peter.vanzandt@lewisbrisbois.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
